assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements rather than separate or individual financial statements.
A dividend receivable arising from an intragroup dividend has been recognised before consolidation adjustments are applied.
The issue is limited to IFRS accounting models evidenced in the provided extracts, especially hedge accounting and consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IFRS 9 requirements on designation of hedged items in consolidated financial statements, including the treatment of intragroup items and foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Consolidation accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IFRS 10 and IAS 24 requirements to eliminate intragroup assets, liabilities, income and expenses in consolidated financial statements, which is directly relevant to an intragroup dividend receivable.

approaches.label: Separate financial statements accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IAS 27 rules under which dividends from a subsidiary are recognised in separate financial statements when the right to receive the dividend is established, providing a distinct IFRS accounting model contrasted with consolidation.
